questions.q: How many great sages are collectively honoured as the Saptarishi in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: The very name 'Saptarishi' means the Seven Seers, denoting the group of seven great rishis.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the Saptarishi are most often described as the mind-born sons of which creator deity?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the Saptarishi as manasaputras, the mind-born sons of Brahma the creator.

questions.q: The Saptarishi are traditionally identified with the seven stars of which well-known constellation?
questions.explanation: According to the Puranas, the seven seers are identified with the seven stars of Ursa Major, the Great Bear.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, the Saptarishi are revered chiefly as the receivers and transmitters of which sacred body of knowledge?
questions.explanation: The seven seers are honoured as the rishis who received and transmitted the Vedas to humanity.

questions.q: Which of these revered sages is included in the commonly cited list of the Saptarishi?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha is one of the seven seers named in the common Puranic list of the Saptarishi.

questions.q: Which sage of the Saptarishi served as the family preceptor (guru) of the Ikshvaku kings, including Rama, in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Vasishtha is the revered family priest and preceptor of the Ikshvaku dynasty of Ayodhya.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which sage of the Saptarishi taught young Rama divine weapons and led him to slay the demoness Tataka?
questions.explanation: Sage Vishvamitra takes Rama and Lakshmana to guard his sacrifice and instructs them in divine weapons in the Ramayana.

questions.q: The era or age presided over by a Manu, during which a particular set of Saptarishi anchor the cosmic order, is called by which name?
questions.explanation: A manvantara is the cosmic period ruled by a Manu, and each manvantara has its own set of Saptarishi who uphold dharma.

questions.q: Which sage of the Saptarishi is renowned as the husband of the devoted Anasuya, celebrated for her chastity in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The sage Atri is the husband of Anasuya, whose great devotion and virtue are praised throughout the Puranas.

questions.q: Which sage among the Saptarishi is described as the father of Parashurama, the warrior-sage avatar of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Jamadagni is the father of Parashurama, the axe-wielding sage and Vishnu avatar described in the Puranas and epics.

questions.q: In the common Puranic list of the Saptarishi, which sage is revered as a progenitor said to have fathered devas, asuras, and many beings through his many consorts?
questions.explanation: Kashyapa is honoured in the Puranas as a great progenitor whose offspring through his many wives include devas, asuras, nagas, and other beings.

questions.q: Which sage of the Saptarishi began as a king named Kaushika before attaining the rank of Brahmarishi through severe austerity?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra was the warrior-king Kaushika who, through long penance, rose to become a Brahmarishi, as told in the Ramayana and Puranas.

questions.q: Sage Vishvamitra is traditionally credited in the Rig Veda with composing the hymn containing which celebrated mantra?
questions.explanation: The sacred Gayatri Mantra of the Rig Veda is traditionally attributed to the seer Vishvamitra.

questions.q: Whose wife Ahalya, in the Ramayana, is restored by the touch of Rama after being cursed, in an episode tied to a sage of the Saptarishi?
questions.explanation: Ahalya is the wife of sage Gautama, and the Ramayana recounts her release from a curse through Rama's grace.

questions.q: Which sage of the Saptarishi hosted Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana at his hermitage near Prayaga during their exile in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, sage Bharadvaja receives Rama and his companions at his ashram near the confluence at Prayaga.

questions.q: The long rivalry over the wish-fulfilling cow Nandini and Kamadhenu in the texts is famously set between Vishvamitra and which other Saptarishi sage?
questions.explanation: The Puranas and Ramayana describe the famed contest between Vishvamitra and Vasishtha over Vasishtha's divine cow.

questions.q: Which deity is said in the Puranas to have been born from the combined essence of Atri, Anasuya, and the divine trinity as the form Dattatreya?
questions.explanation: Dattatreya, embodying Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva, is described in the Puranas as the son of Atri and Anasuya.

questions.q: In the common list of the Saptarishi, the sage Bharadvaja is especially associated with the heritage of which field of sacred learning?
options: The Vedas and the Ayurveda tradition
questions.explanation: Bharadvaja is honoured as a great Vedic seer to whom much sacred and Ayurvedic learning is traditionally ascribed.

questions.q: Which sage of the Saptarishi is traditionally regarded as a Mantra-drashta credited with seeing many hymns of the seventh mandala of the Rig Veda?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha is traditionally credited as the seer of the hymns of the seventh mandala of the Rig Veda.

questions.q: In the Devi or Puranic accounts, the wives of the Saptarishi are themselves identified with the cluster of stars known by which name?
questions.explanation: The wives of the seven seers are associated in the Puranas with the Krittika stars, the cluster known as the Pleiades.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the Saptarishi are not fixed for all time but are renewed for each new cycle ruled by a different what?
questions.explanation: Each manvantara is governed by its own Manu and accompanied by a distinct set of Saptarishi, as the Puranas describe.

questions.q: The sage Vasishtha is said in the Puranas to have a celestial wife whose name is shared with one of the stars of Ursa Major. Who is she?
questions.explanation: Arundhati, the faithful wife of Vasishtha, is identified with the small star companion in the Great Bear constellation.

questions.q: The hermitage of the Saptarishi sage Gautama is traditionally linked to the origin of which major river of the Deccan, also called the Gautami after him?
questions.explanation: Puranic tradition links sage Gautama's hermitage to the source of the Godavari, which is also revered by the name Gautami after the sage.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, after Vishvamitra's long penance, who among the gods finally proclaims him a Brahmarishi, fulfilling his greatest aspiration?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana relates that Vishvamitra attains the title of Brahmarishi, with Brahma and even his rival Vasishtha acknowledging it.

questions.q: The patriarch Kashyapa of the Saptarishi is described in the Puranas as the son of which earlier mind-born sage and Prajapati?
questions.explanation: The Puranas name Kashyapa as the son of the Prajapati Marichi, himself a mind-born son of Brahma.

questions.q: Lists of the Saptarishi vary between texts; in some Puranic enumerations, which sage replaces one of the seven and is named among the seven seers?
questions.explanation: Some Puranic lists name sages such as Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu, Angiras, or Marichi among the seven, as enumerations differ.

questions.q: Which Saptarishi sage is invoked in the Bhagavata Purana as cursing the Yadava youths, an event tied to the eventual decline of the Yadava clan?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that sages including Vishvamitra cursed the mischievous Yadava youths, foreshadowing the clan's ruin.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the river Saraswati is said to have rescued sage Vasishtha when he sought to end his life in grief over the deaths of which kin?
options: His hundred sons, slain through Vishvamitra's enmity
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata recounts that Vasishtha, grieving his sons killed amid his feud with Vishvamitra, was borne to safety by the Saraswati.

questions.q: The sage Jamadagni of the Saptarishi belonged to which renowned rishi lineage, also linked to the seer Bhrigu?
questions.explanation: Jamadagni is counted in the Bhargava lineage descending from Bhrigu, a heritage carried on by his son Parashurama.

questions.q: In Puranic astronomy, the Saptarishi are said to slowly move among the lunar mansions (nakshatras), spending a period of roughly how long in each before advancing?
questions.explanation: Puranic tradition describes the Saptarishi as dwelling about a hundred years in each nakshatra, forming the basis of the Saptarishi calendar era.
